channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit - Space
item.description:
1. Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit  Space
  2. Smile lifts off on quest to reveal Earth’s invisible shield against the solar wind  European Space Agency
  3. SMILE spacecraft launches to capture first X-ray views of Earth's magnetic shield  Phys.org
  4. WATCH: A Spacecraft Is Launching to Study How Earth Survives Solar Storms  ScienceAlert
  5. Europe and China just put a telescope in orbit to photograph something nobody has ever actually seen — the invisible shield keeping the solar wind from stripping Earth bare  Space Daily

item.title: Newly discovered asteroid to pass closer to Earth than the moon Monday - NBC News
item.description:
1. Newly discovered asteroid to pass closer to Earth than the moon Monday  NBC News
  2. An asteroid discovered days ago will narrowly miss Earth  CNN
  3. Watch tonight's asteroid near-miss live online, as the enormous space rock zips past our planet  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—Relatively Speaking  WIRED
  5. Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online TODAY  Space

item.title: Tonight the crescent Moon is between Venus and Jupiter. Here's how to see the stargazing spectacle - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. Tonight the crescent Moon is between Venus and Jupiter. Here's how to see the stargazing spectacle  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

  3. Moon and Planets to Gather in Twilight Spectacle on May 18–20  Sky & Telescope
  4. Venus and a razor-thin moon steal the show after sunset tonight  Space
  5. Venus, moon to share sky for 1 night. How to see it in California  The Desert Sun

item.title: In 1982 the Soviet Union landed a probe on the surface of Venus that survived 127 minutes in heat that melts lead and pressure dense enough to crush a submarine — long enough to scan back two panoramas of flat basaltic rock under an orange-tinted sky b - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1982 the Soviet Union landed a probe on the surface of Venus that survived 127 minutes in heat that melts lead and pressure dense enough to crush a submarine — long enough to scan back two panoramas of flat basaltic rock under an orange-tinted sky b  Space Daily
  2. The mangled remains of probes sent to Venus may still be there  Scientific American
  3. 57 years and one day ago, the Soviet probe Venera 6 traversed the clouds of Venus for 51 minutes under parachutes and stopped transmitting 10 kilometers from the surface because the pressure of 60 bar and the heat of 320 degrees Celsius crushed its 405-  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  4. The Remains of at Least 7 Probes May Still Exist on This Hellish Planet  Gizmodo
  5. The Soviet Venera probes are still sitting on the surface of Venus, slowly being crushed and corroded by 900-degree sulfuric acid clouds, and the photographs they sent back in 1975 remain the only images humans have ever taken from the surface of another  Space Daily

item.title: The moon is moving away from Earth at 3.8 centimetres per year — which means the total solar eclipses humans alive today get to see are something no future civilisation will ever witness - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The moon is moving away from Earth at 3.8 centimetres per year — which means the total solar eclipses humans alive today get to see are something no future civilisation will ever witness  Space Daily
  2. Moon is getting farther away from Earth each year, report finds  The Hill
  3. Solar Eclipses Are Disappearing  artvoice
  4. Total Solar Eclipse Is Disappearing Forever: Why Earth’s Greatest Sky Event Has an Expiration Date  Nbsla.ca
  5. Moon Is Slowly Drifting Away From Earth, Scientists Confirm 3.8 Centimetres a Year in Long-Term Orbital Shift  USA Herald

item.title: Scientists think they’ve cracked the mystery of human right-handedness - ScienceDaily
item.description:

  4. Scientists May Have Finally Solved Why Most Humans Are Right-Handed  ZME Science
  5. Why Did Humans Evolve to Favor One Hand Over the Other?  Nautilus | Science
